content:
On Sunday morning, Finnish air‑space monitoring detected several low‑flying, slow‑moving small objects over sea areas and southeastern Finland. The Finnish Air Force dispatched an F/A‑18 Hornet fighter to the scene to identify the objects. According to the latest information, one unmanned aerial vehicle (drone) crashed north of Kouvola and another crashed east of Kouvola. Police have secured both crash sites for further investigation.  

Defence Minister Antti Häkkänen commented, “Drones have entered Finnish territory. We take this very seriously, and security authorities have responded immediately.”  

The Defence Forces are conducting the investigation in cooperation with other relevant authorities.
